texto: The human in what it is objectively ever since its beginning is two, two who are different. Each part of what constitutes the unity of the human species corresponds to a proper being and a proper Being, to an identity of one's own. In order to carry out the destiny of humanity, the man-human and the woman-human each have to fulfill what they are and at the same time realize the unity that they constitute.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Luce Irigaray sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “The human in what it is objectively ever since its beginning is two, two who are different. Each …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, being, difference, útil em momentos de termino e difference.

frase_original: The human in what it is objectively ever since its beginning is two, two who are different. Each part of what constitutes the unity of the human species corresponds to a proper being and a proper Being, to an identity of one's own. In order to carry out the destiny of humanity, the man-human and the woman-human each have to fulfill what they are and at the same time realize the unity that they constitute.

texto: The ultimate reality from which the path of this becoming could start off again will no longer rest on a ground of 'causa sui.' in any case the sense of a God who would alone be capable of giving an account of self. It is rather from the human and from what the human most irreducibly is that it is a question of starting off again. From the human as it objectively is before it starts to construct a language and a thinking which help to distance it from its beginning, from its prematureness without thinking it in the totality of its being.
explicacao: Citação de Luce Irigaray sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The ultimate reality from which the path of this becoming could start off again will no longer re…” articula reflexao, being, being-in-relation em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e being-in-relation.

texto: Your silence exists as does my self gathering. But so does the almost absolute silence of the world's dawning. In such suspension, before every utterance on earth, there is a cloud, an almost immobile air. The plants already breathe, while we still ask ourselves how to speak to each other, without taking breath away from them.

texto: Your body expresses yesterday in what it wants today. If you think: yesterday I was, tomorrow I shall be, you are thinking: I have died a little. Be what you are becoming, without clinging to what you might have been, what you might yet be. Never settle. Leave definitiveness to the undecided; we don't need it.
explicacao: Citação de Luce Irigaray sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Your body expresses yesterday in what it wants today. If you think: yesterday I was, tomorrow I s…” articula motivacao, coragem, inspiracional em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e inspiracional.

texto: Why only one song, one speech, one text at a time?" - "When Our Lips Speak Together
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Luce Irigaray que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Why only one song, one speech, one text at a time?" - "When Our Lips Speak Together”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, female-sexuality, language, com foco em language.
